challenges.title: Clickable Map
challenges.description: Create an element with a usemap attribute, and a element with a matching name attribute containing at least 2 elements. Each area should have shape, coords, href, and alt attributes. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code: assert ' element' assert 'usemap=' in html.lower(), 'Image should have a usemap attribute' assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the element' import re assert re.search(r']+name=', html.lower()), 'Map should have a name attribute' areas = re.findall(r'= 2, 'Should contain at least 2 elements' assert html.lower().count('shape=') >= 2, 'Each area should have a shape attribute' assert html.lower().count('coords=') >= 2, 'Each area should have a coords attribute' assert html.lower().count('href=') >= 2, 'Each area should have an href attribute'
hints: Link the image to the map:
The map name matches without the #: <map name="mymap">
Each area needs shape, coords, href, and alt attributes

challenges.title: Shape Areas
challenges.description: Create an image map with three <area> elements: one with shape="rect", one with shape="circle", and one with shape="poly". Each area should have coords, href, and alt attributes. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code: assert ' element' assert 'usemap=' in html.lower(), 'Image should have a usemap attribute' assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the element' import re areas = re.findall(r']+>', html.lower()) assert len(areas) >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 elements' shapes = [re.search(r'shape=["\']([^"\']+)["\']', a) for a in areas] shape_values = [m.group(1) for m in shapes if m] assert 'rect' in shape_values, 'Should have an area with shape="rect"' assert 'circle' in shape_values, 'Should have an area with shape="circle"' assert 'poly' in shape_values, 'Should have an area with shape="poly"' assert html.lower().count('coords=') >= 3, 'Each area should have a coords attribute' assert html.lower().count('alt=') >= 3, 'Each area should have an alt attribute'
hints: poly coords: "x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3,..." (each vertex of the polygon)
